idea: cuBLAS reference baseline
Initial baseline: reference blockscale_gemm kernel with WARP_M=64 WARP_N=128 TILE_K=128 WARP_K=32 swizzle128, f32 accumulator
Warp specialization 1p1c with 2-stage pipeline: producer warpgroup handles TMA loads, consumer handles WGMMA compute. Reduced register pressure, improved occupancy
Added __launch_bounds__(256, 2) to force 2 blocks/SM, reducing regs from 153 to ~128 and improving occupancy. 42% perf gain.
3-stage pipeline: more SMEM usage (106KB) hurts occupancy. 2 stages better for this tile size.
Persistent kernel 114 SMs with 4-stage pipeline: too much SMEM, 1 block/SM, tile loop overhead. Non-persistent with 32K blocks better.
WARP_N=256: too much SMEM (144KB, 1 block/SM) and register pressure. N=128 with 2 blocks/SM is optimal.
maxrregcount=128 via nvcc flag causes excessive spilling. __launch_bounds__ in code is better.
Fused mma.row.row.scale in warpspec: generates heavy per-iteration scale overhead in inner loop. Separate mma.scale+mma.commit is far better.
launch_bounds(256,3) requires ~85 regs/thread but WGMMA needs 90+. Cannot achieve 3 blocks/SM with this kernel structure.
1p2c warp spec with TILE_M=128: 384 threads, larger SMEM, only 1 block/SM. 1p1c with launch_bounds(256,2) achieves better occupancy.
WARP_N=64 halved accumulator but doubled grid from 32K to 65K blocks. Worse L2 reuse, higher launch overhead, less work per CTA.
Moved warpgroup_commit+wait before scale_accumulator to ensure WGMMA results ready. No improvement, slightly worse due to pipeline stall.
Block ID swizzle with group_size=8. Improves L2 cache reuse by clustering spatially close tiles onto temporally close blocks.
Reduced swizzle group from 8 to 4. Better L2 reuse for 256x128 grid with tighter grouping.
Swizzle group=2 too tight for 256x128 grid. Excessive row scanning in N.
Swizzle group=6 worse than group=4. Non-power-of-2 creates uneven tile grouping.
launch_bounds(256,1) eliminates spilling but halves occupancy from 2 to 1 block/SM. Net negative.
Scale DMA to SMEM via cute::copy with AsyncCopyAtom crashes with CUDA error 717. Single-thread DMA copy pattern incompatible with scale layout.
Single warpgroup_arrive + commit/wait before scale_accumulator. Slightly worse, compiler already handles ordering.
3-stage pipeline + swizzle. 106KB SMEM per block limits to 1 block/SM. Worse than 2-stage + 2 blocks.
Fast math flag. No measurable improvement - critical path is WGMMA not scalar math.
Swizzle group=3, non-power-of-2 creates uneven groups. Worse than group=4.
L2 promotion (256B) on TMA descriptors. No change — L2 already well-utilized at 88.6%.
maxrregcount=160 without minBlocks. Only 1 block/SM, reduced parallelism.
L1 carveout=50% to help with local memory spills. Too aggressive, reduced available SMEM.
Transposed scale_lhs [DIV_BLK_K,M] for coalesced reads (stride=1). Slightly worse, may conflict with swizzle L2 pattern.
Manually inlined scale_accumulator with __ldg. No change — already __forceinline__.
L2 persistence for scale_lhs. No effect — scale data too large for L2.
Diagonal block scheduling. Verification fails and 152 TFLOPS. Broken mapping.
Swizzle group=5, non-power-of-2 creates uneven tile groups. Worse than group=4.
